author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XII. 12–26.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After our Lord’s raising of one to life, who had been four days dead, to the utter amazement of the Jews, some of whom believed on seeing it, and others perished in their envy, because of that sweet savor which is unto life to some, and to others unto death; after He had sat down to meat with Lazarus—the one who had been dead and raised to life—reclining also at table, and after the pouring on His feet of the ointment which had filled the house with its odor; and after the Jews also had shown their own spiritual abandonment in conceiving the useless cruelty and the monstrously foolish and insane guilt of slaying Lazarus;—of all which we have spoken as we could, by the grace of the Lord, in previous discourses: let your Charity now notice how abundant before our Lord’s passion was the fruit that appeared of His preaching, and how large was the flock of lost sheep of the house of Israel which had heard the Shepherd’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Gospel, the reading of which you have just been listening to, says: “On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem, took branches of palm trees and went forth to meet Him, and cried, Hosanna: blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord as the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The branches of palm trees are laudatory emblems, significant of victory, because the Lord was about to overcome death by dying, and by the trophy of His cross to triumph over the devil, the prince of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The exclamation used by the worshipping people is Hosanna, indicating, as some who know the Hebrew language affirm, rather a state of mind than having any positive significance; just as in our own tongue we have what are called interjections, as when in our grief we say, Alas! or in our joy, Ha! or in our admiration, O how fine! where O! expresses only the feeling of the admirer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the same class must we believe this word to be, as it has failed to find an interpretation both in Greek and Latin, like that other, “Whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this also is allowed to be an interjection, expressive of angry feelings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when it is said, “Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord, [as] the King of Israel,” by “in the name of the Lord” we are rather to understand “in the name of God the Father,” although it might also be understood as in His own name, inasmuch as He is also Himself the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As we find Scripture also saying in another place, “The Lord rained [upon Sodom fire] from the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But His own words are a better guide to our understanding, when He saith, “I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: another will come in his own name, and him ye will receive.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the true teacher of humility is Christ, who humbled Himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He does not lose His divinity in teaching us humility; in the one He is the Father’s equal, in the other He is assimilated to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By that which made Him the equal of the Father, He called us into existence; and by that in which He is like unto us, He redeemed us from ruin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These, then, were the words of praise addressed to Jesus by the multitude, “Hosanna: blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What a cross of mental suffering must the Jewish rulers have endured when they heard so great a multitude proclaiming Christ as their King!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what honor was it to the Lord to be King of Israel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What great thing was it to the King of eternity to become the King of men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ’s kingship over Israel was not for the purpose of exacting tribute, of putting swords into His soldiers’ hands, of subduing His enemies by open warfare; but He was King of Israel in exercising kingly authority over their inward natures, in consulting for their eternal interests, in bringing into His heavenly kingdom those whose faith, and hope, and love were centred in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, for the Son of God, the Father’s equal, the Word by whom all things were made, in His good pleasure to be King of Israel, was an act of condescension and not of promotion; a token of compassion, and not any increase of power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who was called on earth the King of the Jews, is in the heavens the Lord of angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And Jesus, when He had found a young ass, sat thereon.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here the account is briefly given: for how it all happened may be found at full length in the other evangelists.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there is appended to the circumstance itself a testimony from the prophets, to make it evident that He in whom was fulfilled all they read in Scripture, was entirely misunderstood by the evil-minded rulers of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus, then, “found a young ass, and sat thereon; as it is written, Fear not, daughter of Zion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among that people, then, was the daughter of Zion to be found; for Zion is the same as Jerusalem.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among that very people, I say, reprobate and blind as they were, was the daughter of Zion, to whom it was said, “Fear not, daughter of Zion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This daughter of Zion, who was thus divinely addressed, was amongst those sheep that were hearing the Shepherd’s voice, and in that multitude which was celebrating the Lord’s coming with such religious zeal, and accompanying Him in such warlike array.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To her was it said, “Fear not:” acknowledge Him whom thou art now extolling, and give not way to fear when He comes to suffering; for by the shedding of His blood is thy guilt to be blotted out, and thy life restored.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But by the ass’s colt, on which no man had ever sat (for so it is found recorded in the other evangelists), we are to understand the Gentile nations which had not received the law of the Lord; by the ass, on the other hand (for both animals were brought to the Lord), that people of His which came of the nation of Israel, and was already so far subdued as to recognize its Master’s crib.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These things understood not His disciples at the first; but when Jesus was glorified,” that is, when He had manifested the power of His resurrection, “then remembered they that these things were written of Him, and they had done these things unto Him,” that is, they did nothing else but what had been written concerning Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, mentally comparing with the contents of Scripture what was accomplished both prior to and in the course of our Lord’s passion, they found this also therein, that it was in accordance with the utterance of the prophets that He sat on an ass’s colt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The people, therefore, that was with Him when He called Lazarus out of his tomb, and raised him from the dead, bare record.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this cause the crowd also met Him, for that they heard that He had done this miracle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Pharisees, therefore, said among themselves: Perceive ye that we prevail nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, the whole world is gone after Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mob set mob in motion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But why art thou, blinded mob that thou art, filled with envy because the world has gone after its Maker?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And there were certain Gentiles among them that had come up to worship at the feast: the same came therefore to Philip, who was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hearken to the Lord’s reply.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how the Jews wish to kill Him, the Gentiles to see Him; and yet those, too, were of the Jews who cried, “Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, then, were they of the circumcision and they of the uncircumcision, like two house walls running from different directions and meeting together with the kiss of peace, in the one faith of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us listen, then, to the voice of the Cornerstone: “And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come that the Son of man should be glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some one supposes here that He spake of Himself as glorified, because the Gentiles wished to see Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such is not the case.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He saw the Gentiles themselves in all nations coming to the faith after His own passion and resurrection, because, as the apostle says, “Blindness in part has happened to Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles should be come in.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Taking occasion, therefore, from those Gentiles who desired to see Him, He announces the future fullness of the Gentile nations, and promises the near approach of the hour when He should be glorified Himself, and when, on its consummation in heaven, the Gentile nations should be brought to the faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this it is that the prediction pointed, “Be Thou exalted, O God, above the heavens, and Thy glory above all the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such is the fullness of the Gentiles, of which the apostle saith, “Blindness in part is happened to Israel, till the fullness of the Gentiles come in.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the height of His glorification had to be preceded by the depth of His passion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, He went on to add, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, except a grain of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone; but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He spake of Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He Himself was the grain that had to die, and be multiplied; to suffer death through the unbelief of the Jews, and to be multiplied in the faith of many nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now, by way of exhortation to follow in the path of His own passion, He adds, “He that loveth his life shall lose it,” which may be understood in two ways: “He that loveth shall lose,” that is, If thou lovest, be ready to lose; if thou wouldst possess life in Christ, be not afraid of death for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or otherwise, “He that loveth his life shall lose it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not love for fear of losing; love it not here, lest thou lose it in eternity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what I have said last seems better to correspond with the meaning of the Gospel, for there follow the words, “And he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So that when it is said in the previous clause, “He that loveth,” there is to be understood in this world, he it is that shall lose it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But he that hateth,” that is, in this world, is he that shall keep it unto life eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely a profound and strange declaration as to the measure of a man’s love for his own life that leads to its destruction, and of his hatred to it that secures its preservation!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If in a sinful way thou lovest it, then dost thou really hate it; if in a way accordant with what is good thou hast hated it, then hast thou really loved it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Happy they who have so hated their life while keeping it, that their love shall not cause them to lose it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But beware of harboring the notion that thou mayest court self-destruction by any such understanding of thy duty to hate thy life in this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For on such grounds it is that certain wrong-minded and perverted people, who, with regard to themselves, are murderers of a specially cruel and impious character, commit themselves to the flames, suffocate themselves in water, dash themselves against a precipice, and perish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was no teaching of Christ’s, who, on the other hand, met the devil’s suggestion of a precipice with the answer, “Get thee behind me, Satan; for it is written, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Peter also He said, signifying by what death he should glorify God, “When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not;”—where He made it sufficiently plain that it is not by himself but by another that one must be slain who follows in the footsteps of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, when one’s case has reached the crisis that this condition is placed before him, either that he must act contrary to the divine commandment or quit this life, and that a man is compelled to choose one or other of the two by the persecutor who is threatening him with death, in such circumstances let him prefer dying in the love of God to living under His anger, in such circumstances let him hate his life in this world that he may keep it unto life eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If any man serve me, let him follow me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that, “let him follow me,” but just, let him imitate me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because Christ suffered for us,” says the Apostle Peter, “leaving us an example that we should follow His steps.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have the meaning of the words, “If any man serve me, let him follow me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But with what result? what wages? what reward?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And where I am,” He says, “there shall also my servant be.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him be freely loved, that so the reward of the service done Him may be to be with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where will one be well apart from Him, or when will one come to feel himself in an evil case in company with Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear it still more plainly: “If any man serve me, him will my Father honor.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what will be the honor but to be with His Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of what He said before, “Where I am, there shall also my servant be,” we may understand Him as giving the explanation, when He says here, “him will my Father honor.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what greater honor can await an adopted son than to be with the Only-begotten; not, indeed, as raised to the level of His Godhead, but made a partaker of His eternity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it becomes us rather to inquire what is to be understood by this serving of Christ to which there is attached so great a reward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we have taken up the idea that the serving of Christ is the preparation of what is needful for the body, or the cooking and serving up of food, or the mixing of drink and handing the cup to one at the supper table; this, indeed, was done to Him by those who had the privilege of His bodily presence, as in the case of Martha and Mary, when Lazarus also was one of those who sat at the table.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in that sort of way Christ was served also by the reprobate Judas; for it was he also who had the money bag; and although he had the exceeding wickedness to steal of its contents, yet it was he also who provided what was needful for the meal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so also, when our Lord said to him, “What thou doest, do quickly,” there were some who thought that He only gave him orders to make some needful preparations for the feast-day, or to give something to the poor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In no sense, therefore, was it of this class of servants that the Lord said, “Where I am, there shall also my servant be,” and “If any man serve me, him will my Father honor;” for we see that Judas, who served in this way, became an object of reprobation rather than of honor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, go elsewhere to find out what this serving of Christ implies, and not rather see its disclosure in the words themselves? for when He said, “If any man serve me, let him follow me,” He wished it to be understood just as if He had said, If any man doth not follow me, he serveth me not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And those, therefore, are the servants of Jesus Christ, who seek not their own things, but the things that are Jesus Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “let him follow me” is just this: Let him walk in my ways, and not in his own; as it is written elsewhere, “He that saith he abideth in Christ, ought himself also so to walk, even as He walked.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he ought, if supplying food to the hungry, to do it in the way of mercy and not of boasting, seeking therein nothing else but the doing of good, and not letting his left hand know what his right hand doeth; in other words, that all thought of self-seeking should be utterly estranged from a work of charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that serveth in this way serveth Christ, and will have it rightly said to him, “Inasmuch as ye did it unto one of the least of those who are mine, ye did it unto me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus doing not only those acts of mercy that pertain to the body, but every good work, for the sake of Christ (for then will all be good, because “Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth”), he is Christ’s servant even to that work of special love, which is to lay down his life for the brethren, for that were to lay it down also for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this also will He say hereafter in behalf of His members: Inasmuch as ye did it for these, ye have done it for me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And certainly it was in reference to such a work that He was also pleased to make and to style Himself a servant, when He says, “Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto [served], but to minister [serve], and to lay down His life for many.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every one, therefore, is the servant of Christ in the same way as Christ also is a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he that serveth Christ in this way will be honored by His Father with the signal honor of being with His Son, and having nothing wanting to his happiness for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, brethren, when you hear the Lord saying, “Where I am, there shall also my servant be,” do not think merely of good bishops and clergymen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But be yourselves also in your own way serving Christ, by good lives, by giving alms, by preaching His name and doctrine as you can; and every father of a family also, be acknowledging in this name the affection he owes as a parent to his family.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ’s sake, and for the sake of life eternal, let him be warning, and teaching, and exhorting, and correcting all his household; let him show kindliness, and exercise discipline; and so in his own house he will be filling an ecclesiastical and kind of episcopal office, and serving Christ, that he may be with Him for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even that noblest service of suffering has been rendered by many of your class; for many who were neither bishops nor clergy, but young men and virgins, those advanced in years with those who were not, many married persons both male and female, many fathers and mothers of families, have served Christ even to the laying down of their lives in martyrdom for His sake, and have been honored by the Father in receiving crowns of exceeding glory.
